course_title: Honours Coursework Topics in Molecular Biosciences

learning_outcomes.description: Apply advanced knowledge and problem-solving skills in your area of specialisation (Biochemistry, Molecular Biology, Microbiology or Genetics).
Apply advanced knowledge of research techniques relevant to aspects of your research work.
Communicate advanced knowledge of research techniques relevant in your area of specialisation.

assessments.task_description:
The oral assessment task will involve a 20-minute PowerPoint presentation on your selected paper, followed by a 20-minute question period.

  • The slides and notes for the presentation need to be submitted as a PDF through Turnitin on the day of the presentation. The slides and notes will not be marked, but are for reference purposes only.
  • Detailed instructions for this assessment item will be made available on Blackboard, together with a schedule for presentations.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Slides and Notes through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
During your Journal Club sessions you will be assessed based on willingness to ask questions and your overall contribution to any discussion. You must read the paper to be presented prior to each session to enable you to ask relevant questions that focus on critical issues of the study, presentation or overall research theme.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: There is no submission as this assessment item consists of participation during the weekly meetings.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You will prepare a presentation on an experimental technique of your choosing that is relevant to your Honours research project. The presentation should review the history, development, underlying mechanisms, uses, strengths and deficiencies, and future directions of your chosen technique.

Presentation duration: 20 minutes, followed by 20 minutes for questions.

  • The mark for this assessment item will consist of a 25% mark assigned by the session coordinator and a 5% peer mark collated from audience members' marks. The peer mark will be an average of the available marks, with the coordinator reserving the right to exclude extreme outliers (more than 10 marks away from the average, up or down) from the peer mark average.
  • The slides and notes for the presentation need to be submitted as a PDF through Turnitin on the day of the presentation. The slides and notes will not be marked, but are for reference purposes only.
  • Detailed instructions for this assessment item will be made available on Blackboard. A schedule for presentations will be made available on Blackboard at the start of the semester.


Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Slides and Notes through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Participation in discussion of Experimental Techniques
assessments.task_description:
During your presentations of experimental techniques you will be assessed based on willingness to ask questions and your overall contribution to any discussion. You should look into basic aspects of the method prior to each session to enable you to ask relevant questions that focus on critical aspects of this technique, presentation or overall relevance to the research topic this method is to be applied in.

As part of your participation mark, you will be required to complete peer-marking evaluations of the presentations, which will count 5% towards the presenter's presentation mark (see above - marks for presentations)


Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: During each session that you attend, you will complete a peer-marking form for the presentations that are given in the session.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
